sections.paragraph:
ἀνορϑόω [ἀνά, ὀρϑόω ‘set straight’ fr. ὀρθός] ‘cause to be in an earlier upright or strong condition’

—a. of someth. in a collapsed condition, set up again σϰηνὴ Δαυίδ tent of David Ac 15:16.

—b. of pers. suffering affliction, straighten up, of a woman who was bent over: ἀνωρϑώϑη (aor. pass. with active force) she stood upright Lk 13:13; in imagery of infirm hands and knees strengthen Hb 12:12.
